themes: Tre'Davious White vs. DeAndre Hopkins matchup
Bills second playoff berth in three years
writeup: New Year's Day 2020 found the Buffalo Bills squarely in the national spotlight as the football world turned its attention to their upcoming AFC Wild Card showdown with the Houston Texans, set for January 4 at NRG Stadium. The storylines were compelling: Josh Allen was poised to start his first-ever playoff game, carrying the burden and excitement of being the first Bills quarterback since Jim Kelly to have a shot at a postseason victory. All eyes were also on the marquee individual matchup of the week — All-Pro cornerback Tre'Davious White, fresh off a six-interception season, squaring off against the supremely talented DeAndre Hopkins, with Hopkins already firing shots on social media. Analysts across the country were weighing Buffalo's stout defense — which surrendered the second-fewest points per game in the NFL — against the unpredictability of Deshaun Watson and a Texans offense that had questions surrounding the health of J.J. Watt and Will Fuller. As Bills Mafia began descending on Houston, a city buzzing with playoff energy, the consensus was clear: this was a toss-up game, and Buffalo had every reason to believe.
